title: sisymbrium irio subsp. costei
intro: Sisymbrium irio subsp. costei, also known as Jointed Charlock or Jointed Poppy-mustard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its seeds, which contain a compound called sinalbin, a glucosinolate that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Sisymbrium irio subsp. costei include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its seeds, which can be used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
